title: Epidemiology with pkmapr
text: Gilgit-Baltistan Azad Kashmir share land boundaries provinces. standard contiguity rules, zero neighbours — breaking spatial statistics. pk_neighbors() handles automatically.

title: Epidemiology with pkmapr
text: returned object contains: - w$nb: neighbours list - w$listw: row-standardised weights (ready spdep)
code: districts <- get_districts() w <- pk_neighbors(districts, disputed = "exclude")

title: Epidemiology with pkmapr
text: significant p-value indicates spatial clustering.
code: set.seed(2023)  # Create spatially autocorrelated synthetic data n <- nrow(districts) rho <- 0.6 W_mat <- listw2mat(w$listw) epsilon <- rnorm(n, mean = 50, sd = 10) y <- as.numeric(solve(diag(n) - rho * W_mat) %*% epsilon)  districts$synthetic_rate <- y  # Test for spatial autocorrelation moran_result <- moran.test(districts$synthetic_rate, w$listw) print(moran_result)

title: Epidemiology with pkmapr
text: Identify local clusters outliers:
code: # Calculate local Moran's I lisa <- localmoran(districts$synthetic_rate, w$listw)  # Add results to districts districts$lisa_i <- lisa[, 1]  # local I districts$lisa_p <- lisa[, 5]  # p-value  # Classify clusters (simplified) districts$cluster <- case_when(   districts$lisa_p > 0.05 ~ "Not significant",   districts$synthetic_rate > mean(districts$synthetic_rate) &     districts$lisa_i > 0 ~ "High-High",   districts$synthetic_rate < mean(districts$synthetic_rate) &     districts$lisa_i > 0 ~ "Low-Low",   districts$lisa_i < 0 ~ "Outlier",   TRUE ~ "Other" )  # Map the clusters ggplot2::ggplot(districts) +   ggplot2::geom_sf(ggplot2::aes(fill = cluster)) +   ggplot2::theme_void() +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Spatial Clusters")

title: Epidemiology with pkmapr
code: # Calculate Gi* for the synthetic data gi_star <- localG(districts$synthetic_rate, w$listw)  districts$gi_star <- as.numeric(gi_star) districts$hotspot <- ifelse(districts$gi_star > 1.96, "Hotspot",                             ifelse(districts$gi_star < -1.96, "Coldspot", "Not significant"))  pk_map(districts, fill = "hotspot", title = "Hotspots (p < 0.05)")

title: Epidemiology with pkmapr
text: Line Control creates analytical ambiguity. pk_neighbors() makes decision explicit:
code: # Exclude (default) — GB/AJK get nearest neighbour fallback w_exclude <- pk_neighbors(districts, disputed = "exclude")  # Include — treat disputed boundaries as shared w_include <- pk_neighbors(districts, disputed = "include")  # Flag — document which units are affected w_flag <- pk_neighbors(districts, disputed = "flag") print(w_flag$boundary_note)

title: Epidemiology with pkmapr
code: # 1. Get data districts <- get_districts()  # 2. Build weights with explicit dispute handling w <- pk_neighbors(districts, disputed = "exclude")  # 3. Attach your real case data (replace with your own) # districts <- pk_join(districts, your_case_data, by = "district_code")  # 4. Calculate rates (example) # districts <- districts |> #   mutate(rate = cases / population * 100000)  # 5. Test for spatial autocorrelation # moran.test(districts$rate, w$listw)  # 6. Identify clusters # lisa <- localmoran(districts$rate, w$listw) # districts$lisa_cluster <- attr(lisa, "quadr")$mean  # 7. Map results # pk_map(districts, fill = "lisa_cluster")

title: Epidemiology with pkmapr
text: Bivand, R. S., Pebesma, E., & Gomez-Rubio, V. (2013). Applied spatial data analysis R. Springer. OCHA Pakistan. (2023). COD-AB Administrative Boundaries.

title: Introduction to pkmapr
text: Install pkmapr GitHub: :
code: remotes::install_github("abdullahumer1101/pkmapr") # install.packages("pkmapr", repos = "https://abdullahumer1101.r-universe.dev")

title: Introduction to pkmapr
text: Get province boundaries create quick map:
code: provinces <- get_provinces() pk_map(provinces)

title: Introduction to pkmapr
text: Always check official names filtering joining:
code: # All provinces with their codes pk_dictionary("provinces")  # Districts in Punjab pk_dictionary("districts", province = "Punjab")  # Tehsils in Lahore district pk_dictionary("tehsils", district = "Lahore")

title: Introduction to pkmapr
code: library(dplyr)  # Example: district-level data my_data <- data.frame(   district_code = c("PK603", "PK604"),   value = c(42, 37) )  districts <- get_districts() |>   pk_join(my_data, by = "district_code")  # Map the result pk_map(districts, fill = "value", title = "My Values")

title: Introduction to pkmapr
code: pk_map_interactive(districts,                      fill = "value",                     popup = c("district_name", "value"))

title: Introduction to pkmapr
text: vignette("spatial-analysis-pkmapr") buffers, centroids, point--polygon vignette("epidemiology-pkmapr") spatial autocorrelation hotspots

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: Convert polygons points labeling /distance calculations:
code: districts <- get_districts() centroids <- pk_centroid(districts)  # Map districts with centroid points pk_map(districts) +   ggplot2::geom_sf(data = centroids, color = "red", size = 0.5)

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: Create buffer zones around administrative units (distances km):
code: # 10km buffer around Lahore district lahore <- get_districts(province = "Punjab") |>   filter(district_name == "Lahore")  lahore_buffer <- pk_buffer(lahore, dist_km = 10)  pk_map(lahore_buffer, title = "Lahore buffer") +   ggplot2::geom_sf(data = lahore, fill = "red", alpha = 0.5)

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: Compute distances units:
code: # Distance matrix between provinces (centroid to centroid) provinces <- get_provinces() dist_matrix <- pk_distance(provinces, provinces)  # Distance from each province to Karachi karachi <- get_districts(province = "Sindh") |>   filter(district_name == "Karachi")  distances <- pk_distance(provinces, karachi)

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: Assign GPS points administrative units:
code: # Example: health facility locations facilities <- data.frame(   name = c("Hospital A", "Clinic B"),   lon = c(74.3, 74.5),   lat = c(31.5, 31.6) ) |>   sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)  # Assign to districts facilities_with_district <- pk_points_in(facilities, districts)  # View result facilities_with_district |>   sf::st_drop_geometry() |>   select(name, district_name)

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: Aggregate finer units coarser levels:
code: # Dissolve tehsils to district level tehsils <- get_tehsils() districts_from_tehsils <- pk_union(tehsils, by = "district_name")  # Compare area original_districts <- get_districts() pk_area(original_districts) pk_area(districts_from_tehsils)  # Should be similar

title: Spatial Analysis with pkmapr
text: WGS84 (default) measures degrees, measurements using projected CRS:
code: # Recommended CRS for your data's extent pk_crs_suggest(get_districts(province = "Punjab"))  # Reproject for metric operations districts_utm <- pk_project(districts, crs = 32642)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Abdullah Umer. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Abdullah Umer (2026). pkmapr: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit. R package version 1.2.0, https://abdullahumer1101.github.io/pkmapr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {pkmapr: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit},   author = {{Abdullah Umer}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.2.0},   url = {https://abdullahumer1101.github.io/pkmapr/}, }

title: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit
text: pkmapr (pronounced P-K-Mapper) provides clean, tidy interface Pakistan’s official administrative boundary data OCHA/HDX. includes geometries country, province, district, tehsil level sf objects compatible tidyverse geospatial ecosystem, includes utilities geographic dictionary lookup, spatial measurement, neighbour structure construction use spdep, ggplot2, leaflet, related packages.

title: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit
code: # GitHub remotes::install_github("abdullahumer1101/pkmapr")  # R-Universe install.packages("pkmapr", repos = "https://abdullahumer1101.r-universe.dev")

title: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit
text: cite directly: Umer, . (2025). pkmapr: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19769542
code: citation("pkmapr")

title: Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pk_crs_suggest
text: Examines geographic extent sf object recommends appropriate projected coordinate reference system metric operations. Pakistan spans UTM zones 41N, 42N, 43N; national-level analyses benefit equal-area projection.

title: Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pk_crs_suggest
text: named list: epsg Integer EPSG code recommended CRS. name Human-readable CRS name. rationale One-sentence explanation recommendation.

title: Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pk_crs_suggest
text: Using WGS84 (EPSG:4326) distance, area, buffer operations produces inaccurate results measures degrees rather metres.

title: Suggest an appropriate projected CRS for a Pakistan sf object — pk_crs_suggest

what: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pk_neighbors
title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pk_neighbors
text: Builds contiguity distance-based spatial neighbours structure direct use spdep spatialreg. Handles Pakistan-specific complexities including non-contiguous units disputed boundaries.

title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pk_neighbors
code: pk_neighbors(   x,   style = c("queen", "rook", "knn"),   k = NULL,   disputed = c("exclude", "include", "flag") )

title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pk_neighbors
text: x sf object polygon geometries. style Character. Neighbour definition: "queen" (shared boundary point, default), "rook" (shared edge), "knn" (k nearest centroids). k Integer. Number nearest neighbours. Required style = "knn". disputed Character. Treatment non-contiguous units disputed boundaries: exclude Default. Non-contiguous units receive one nearest neighbour fallback. informative message identifies affected units. include Treat boundaries normal shared boundaries. flag Include boundaries add boundary_note element result documenting units affected.

title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pk_neighbors
text: named list: nb spdep nb object. listw row-standardised spdep listw object, ready spdep::moran.test(), spdep::localMoran(), spatialreg::lagsarlm(), related functions. boundary_note Character. Present disputed = "flag".

title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pk_neighbors
text: Gilgit-Baltistan Azad Kashmir might break spatial statistics. disputed argument controls Line Control special administrative boundaries flagged OCHA source data treated, making analytical decision explicit reproducible.

title: Construct a spatial neighbours list for Pakistan administrative units — pk_neighbors
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive()   districts <- get_districts()   w <- pk_neighbors(districts)    # Calculate Moran's I using spdep   moran_result <- spdep::moran.test(districts$area_km2, w$listw) }

title: Assign points to administrative units (point-in-polygon) — pk_points_in

title: Assign points to administrative units (point-in-polygon) — pk_points_in

title: Assign points to administrative units (point-in-polygon) — pk_points_in
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive()   # Get district boundaries   districts <- get_districts()    # Create sample points (or use your own sf object)   set.seed(123)   sample_points <- sf::st_sample(districts, size = 50)   sample_points_sf <- sf::st_sf(geometry = sample_points)    # Assign points to districts   points_with_districts <- pk_points_in(sample_points_sf, districts)   print(head(points_with_districts)) }

text: matching pk_search() case-insensitive default. "Lahore", "lahore", "LAHORE" return results.

title: Search Across All Administrative Levels — pk_search
text: fuzzy = TRUE, function uses approximate string matching handle typos spelling variations. example, "lahor" match "Lahore". useful interactive exploration may return unexpected results ambiguous queries.

title: Search Across All Administrative Levels — pk_search
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Case-insensitive search   pk_search("lahore")   # Returns Lahore district and tehsils   pk_search("LAHORE")   # Same result    # Fuzzy search for misspelled "lahore"   pk_search("lahor", fuzzy = TRUE)   pk_search("lahre", fuzzy = TRUE)    # Search by code   pk_search("PK6") } # }

what: pkmapr: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit — pkmapr
title: pkmapr: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit — pkmapr
text: Provides tidy interface Pakistan's official administrative boundary data United Nations Office Coordination Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA). Downloads caches spatial data country, province, district, tehsil levels sf objects compatible tidyverse geospatial ecosystem.

title: pkmapr: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit — pkmapr
text: Data loading get_districts, get_tehsils, get_provinces, get_country: Download administrative boundaries different levels. Lookup pk_dictionary: Look official names PBS geocodes. Joining pk_join: Join external data administrative units, match checking. Spatial weights pk_neighbors: Build spatial weights spdep spatialreg. Visualisation pk_map, pk_map_interactive: Quick choropleth maps ggplot2 leaflet.

title: pkmapr: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit — pkmapr
text: pkmapr uses following global options: pkmapr_use_cache Logical. TRUE, caches downloaded data persistent user directory. Default FALSE (uses temporary directory). pkmapr_refresh Logical. Re-download data even cached copy exists. Default FALSE.

title: pkmapr: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit — pkmapr
text: United Nations Office Coordination Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA). Pakistan Administrative Boundaries. https://data.humdata.org/

title: pkmapr: Pakistan Spatial Data Toolkit — pkmapr
text: Maintainer: Abdullah Umer abdullahumer1101@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: pkmapr v1.2.0
text: Added extensive tests Test coverage now ~80%

title: pkmapr v1.1.1
text: functional changes. Added single quote marks programs description.

title: pkmapr v1.1.0
text: Removed simplified parameter (data now always simplified version). Removed refresh parameter (data now embedded package, downloading). Removed pkmapr_cache_info() pkmapr_clear_cache(). Package size reduced.

title: pkmapr v1.0.0
text: Testing edge cases. First stable release.

title: pkmapr v0.3.0
text: New functions: pk_version() checks installed version latest GitHub release, pk_search() searches across admin levels optional fuzzy matching Enhancements: Province district filters now case-insensitive, error messages show valid options filter fails, whitespace automatically trimmed filter inputs Documentation: Added notes case insensitivity, fuzzy matching, also updated examples show case-insensitive usage Dependencies: Added jsonlite Imports (used pk_version())

title: pkmapr 0.2.0
text: Switched licence MIT GPL-3. Improved formatting rendering pkgdown site. Expanded test coverage core functions. Improved vignettes function documentation.

title: pkmapr 0.1.
text: Initial release.
